archetypes.name: Fake AI Trap
archetypes.pattern: Human labor disguised as AI; no genuine automation
archetypes.inversion: Verifiable AI-native operations with transparent automation metrics

archetypes.name: Thin Wrapper Syndrome
archetypes.pattern: No proprietary data; easily replicated by foundational models
archetypes.inversion: Deep vertical AI with domain-specific data flywheels

archetypes.name: Hardware Scaling Chasm
archetypes.inversion: Asset-light hardware intelligence (software that optimizes hardware)

archetypes.name: Unsustainable Unit Economics
archetypes.pattern: Growth funded losses; no path to contribution margin
archetypes.inversion: Outcome-based pricing with AI-driven cost structure optimization

archetypes.name: Regulatory Blind Spot
archetypes.pattern: Operating in legal gray areas; targeting vulnerable users
archetypes.inversion: Compliance-first architecture; regulatory moat as competitive advantage

archetypes.name: Commoditization Collapse
archetypes.pattern: Adjacent to hyperscalers or foundational models
archetypes.inversion: Niche vertical depth where hyperscalers won't go

archetypes.name: Market Timing Failure
archetypes.pattern: Built on temporary demand; no durable behavioral shift
archetypes.inversion: Identify permanent behavioral changes accelerated by AI adoption

archetypes.name: Governance Failure
archetypes.pattern: Poor financial controls; lack of investor transparency
archetypes.inversion: AI-native financial governance as a product feature

failures.name: Builder.ai
failures.cause: Business model fraud — human labor disguised as AI; lack of financial governance
failures.lesson: Authenticity in AI is existential; governance is infrastructure
failures.opportunity: Genuine AI-native software delivery platforms with transparent automation metrics

failures.name: Adept AI
failures.cause: Commoditization by foundational models; no proprietary data moat; capital intensity
failures.lesson: Thin AI wrappers have no moat; vertical depth + proprietary data is the only defense
failures.opportunity: Deep-vertical agentic AI with domain-specific training data and workflow lock-in

failures.name: Jasper AI
failures.cause: Rapid commoditization by GPT-4/Claude; no retention loop; no workflow moat
failures.lesson: Content generation is a feature, not a company; moat must be workflow, not output
failures.opportunity: AI-native content operations platforms with memory, brand voice, and distribution integration

failures.name: Lilium
failures.cause: Hardware scaling chasm; regulatory delays; capital intensity exceeded projections
failures.lesson: Hardware requires patient capital and realistic timelines; R&D ≠ commercialization
failures.opportunity: Urban air mobility infrastructure (vertiports, air traffic management, charging networks)

failures.name: Northvolt
failures.cause: Manufacturing execution failure; scaling complexity underestimated
failures.lesson: Deep manufacturing requires operational excellence, not just engineering vision
failures.opportunity: Battery recycling, second-life battery systems, and AI-driven manufacturing optimization

failures.name: Vroom
failures.cause: High operational costs; inventory management failure; macroeconomic headwinds
failures.lesson: Disrupting physical asset markets requires operational mastery, not just digital UX
failures.opportunity: AI-native used vehicle inspection, pricing, and financing platforms (asset-light)

failures.name: Convoy
failures.cause: Low-margin industry; intense competition; unit economics never worked
failures.lesson: Marketplace businesses in commoditized industries require network density before profitability
failures.opportunity: Niche freight intelligence — specialized cargo (temperature-controlled, hazmat, last-mile)

failures.name: Getir
failures.cause: Unsustainable unit economics; over-expansion; delivery cost structure unworkable
failures.lesson: Speed and convenience do not guarantee profitability; density is everything in delivery
failures.opportunity: Hyperlocal inventory intelligence and dark store optimization platforms

failures.name: Dave
failures.cause: Intense competition; difficulty achieving profitability; commoditized product
failures.lesson: Neobanking without a clear moat (community, data, vertical) is a race to zero
failures.opportunity: Vertical neobanks for specific professions (gig workers, freelancers, tradespeople) with embedded insurance and tax

failures.name: EasyKnock
failures.cause: Legally questionable sale-leaseback model; predatory practices; regulatory collapse
failures.lesson: Business models targeting vulnerable populations face existential regulatory risk
failures.opportunity: Ethical home equity access platforms with transparent, consumer-protective structures

failures.name: Sprinklr
failures.cause: Failure to demonstrate clear ROI; complex product; market consolidation
failures.lesson: Enterprise SaaS must prove ROI in 90 days or face churn; complexity kills adoption
failures.opportunity: AI-native social intelligence platforms with automated ROI dashboards and outcome-based pricing

failures.name: Sumo Logic
failures.cause: Intense competition from AWS/Datadog; difficulty scaling profitably
failures.lesson: Infrastructure SaaS adjacent to hyperscalers is a dangerous position
failures.opportunity: AI-native observability for edge computing and IoT — outside hyperscaler territory

failures.name: Fastly
failures.cause: Commoditization by AWS CloudFront, Cloudflare; pricing pressure
failures.lesson: Edge infrastructure is a utility; value must be in the intelligence layer above it
failures.opportunity: AI inference at the edge — low-latency AI processing for real-time applications

failures.name: Canoo
failures.cause: Production scaling failure; high cash burn; execution gap
failures.lesson: EV manufacturing requires automotive-grade operational discipline
failures.opportunity: EV fleet management software and charging infrastructure optimization (asset-light)

failures.name: Rain AI
failures.cause: Semiconductor fabrication complexity; prototype-to-production gap
failures.lesson: Chip design requires decades of expertise; software-defined approaches are more viable
failures.opportunity: AI inference optimization software — achieving hardware-like performance through software

failures.name: Byju's
failures.cause: Unsustainable growth; lack of financial transparency; post-COVID demand collapse
failures.lesson: EdTech must prove learning outcomes, not just engagement; governance is non-negotiable
failures.opportunity: AI-native adaptive learning with measurable outcome guarantees and employer integration

failures.name: Plenty Unlimited
failures.cause: Unsustainable unit economics; energy costs exceeded projections
failures.lesson: Indoor farming economics require energy breakthroughs; software-first AgTech is more viable
failures.opportunity: AI-driven precision agriculture for outdoor farming — soil intelligence, yield optimization

failures.name: Pandion
failures.cause: Market timing failure; pandemic boom collapsed; VC funding dried up
failures.lesson: Timing is a moat; businesses built on temporary demand spikes are fragile
failures.opportunity: Returns logistics optimization — the $800B reverse logistics problem remains unsolved

failures.name: Cushion
failures.cause: Market too small; business model not venture-scale
failures.lesson: Real problems don't always equal venture-scale markets; TAM validation is critical
failures.opportunity: Embedded financial advocacy — AI agents that negotiate fees, rates, and terms on behalf of consumers at scale

failures.name: Babylon Health
failures.cause: Regulatory complexity; overpromised AI capabilities; unit economics; market access
failures.lesson: Healthcare AI must be clinically validated, not just technologically impressive
failures.opportunity: AI-native care navigation platforms that augment (not replace) clinical decision-making
